questions.question_text: Iowa real estate licenses must be renewed on what cycle?
questions.explanation: Iowa uses a 3-year renewal cycle with a December 31 deadline. Licensees must complete 36 hours of continuing education during each 3-year period to qualify for renewal.

questions.question_text: A licensee is planning her continuing education for the renewal period. How many total CE hours are required?
questions.explanation: Iowa requires 36 hours of continuing education per 3-year renewal cycle. This must include the 8-hour Law Update, 4-hour Ethics course, and 24 hours of elective courses.

questions.question_text: A licensee completed 30 elective CE hours this cycle but only needs 24. How many unused elective hours may carry over to the next cycle?
questions.explanation: Iowa allows up to 18 unused elective hours to carry over to the next renewal cycle. Since this licensee has 6 excess elective hours (30 minus 24), all 6 carry over because that is under the 18-hour cap.

questions.question_text: A licensee plans to take all 36 CE hours online. Why will this plan NOT satisfy Iowa requirements?
questions.correct_answer: At least 12 hours must be live
distractors: All 36 hours must be in person
questions.explanation: Iowa requires a minimum of 12 hours of live (synchronous) instruction during each renewal cycle. The maximum distance education allowed is 24 hours. The licensee must attend at least 12 hours in person or via live webinar.

questions.question_text: Which mandatory CE courses must every Iowa licensee complete each renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Every Iowa licensee must complete the 8-hour Law Update course and the 4-hour Ethics course each renewal cycle. The remaining 24 hours are elective courses chosen by the licensee from approved offerings.

questions.question_text: A licensee places her license on inactive status. Which statement is correct about her obligations?
questions.correct_answer: She must still complete CE requirements
distractors: She is exempt from all CE
questions.explanation: Inactive licensees in Iowa cannot practice real estate but must still complete continuing education requirements to maintain their license. Failure to complete CE will cause the license to lapse.

questions.question_text: What distinguishes an active license from an inactive license in Iowa?
questions.correct_answer: Active allows practice; inactive does not
distractors: Active requires CE; inactive does not
Active requires E&O; inactive does not
questions.explanation: An active Iowa license permits the holder to practice real estate under a broker or independently (if a broker). An inactive license means the person is licensed but may not engage in any licensed real estate activity. Both require CE.

questions.question_text: A licensee forgot to renew and her license has lapsed. What must she do to practice again?
distractors: Simply pay the renewal fee late
questions.explanation: When an Iowa license lapses or expires, the licensee must complete a reinstatement process. This differs from a simple late renewal and may require additional education, fees, or examination depending on how long the license has been lapsed.

questions.question_text: A broker notices a salesperson on her team has been showing homes despite having an inactive license. What is the risk?
distractors: No risk if supervised by a broker
questions.explanation: An inactive licensee who engages in real estate activities is practicing without a valid license, which is a serious violation. Both the salesperson and the supervising broker may face disciplinary action from the Commission.

questions.question_text: An Iowa licensee wants to switch from inactive to active status. What must she have in place?
questions.correct_answer: A sponsoring broker and current CE
distractors: Only a sponsoring broker is needed
Only proof of current CE completion
Must retake the licensing exam first
questions.explanation: To reactivate an Iowa license, a salesperson must have a sponsoring broker willing to supervise them and must have current continuing education completed. The Commission processes the status change once requirements are met.

questions.question_text: What type of insurance must all active Iowa real estate licensees maintain?
questions.explanation: Iowa requires all active licensees to carry errors and omissions (E&O) insurance. Proof of coverage must be submitted with the license application. Failure to maintain E&O insurance results in automatic suspension of the license.

questions.question_text: A licensee's E&O insurance policy lapses and she fails to obtain new coverage. What happens to her license?
distractors: She pays a fine but keeps practicing
questions.explanation: Iowa law mandates E&O insurance for all active licensees. If coverage lapses, the license is automatically suspended. The licensee cannot practice until she obtains new E&O coverage and the Commission lifts the suspension.

questions.question_text: A salesperson is transferring from Broker A to Broker B. When can she begin working under Broker B?
questions.correct_answer: After the Commission processes the transfer
distractors: Immediately upon signing with Broker B
Only at the start of the next month
questions.explanation: When transferring between brokers in Iowa, the licensee must notify the Commission and the new broker must file the transfer paperwork. The salesperson cannot practice under the new broker until the Commission has processed the transfer.

questions.question_text: During a license transfer, who is responsible for filing the transfer paperwork with the Commission?
questions.explanation: When a salesperson transfers between brokers in Iowa, the new broker files the transfer paperwork with the Commission. The salesperson must also notify the Commission, but the new broker initiates the formal transfer process.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed salesperson submits her application without proof of E&O insurance. What is the likely outcome?
questions.correct_answer: The application will not be approved
distractors: She may practice under broker coverage
questions.explanation: Iowa requires proof of E&O insurance with the license application. Without it, the application cannot be approved. The applicant must obtain E&O coverage and submit proof before the Commission will issue the license.
